speaker: DUKE
text: Hail to you, provost, so I think you are.

speaker: PROVOST
text: I am the Provost. What’s your will, good friar?

speaker: DUKE
text: Bound by my charity and my blest order,

speaker: DUKE
text: I come to visit the afflicted spirits

speaker: DUKE
text: Here in the prison. Do me the common right

speaker: DUKE
text: To let me see them, and to make me know

speaker: DUKE
text: The nature of their crimes, that I may minister

speaker: DUKE
text: To them accordingly.

speaker: PROVOST
text: I would do more than that if more were needful.

speaker: PROVOST
text: Look, here comes one, a gentlewoman of mine,

speaker: PROVOST
text: Who, falling in the flaws of her own youth,

speaker: PROVOST
text: Hath blistered her report. She is with child,

speaker: PROVOST
text: And he that got it, sentenced—a young man,

speaker: PROVOST
text: More fit to do another such offense

speaker: PROVOST
text: Than die for this.

speaker: DUKE
text: When must he die?

speaker: PROVOST
text: As I do think, tomorrow.

speaker: PROVOST
text:
To
                 
                Juliet
                .
                 
              I have provided for you. Stay awhile

speaker: PROVOST
text: And you shall be conducted.

speaker: DUKE
text: Repent you, fair one, of the sin you carry?

speaker: JULIET
text: I do; and bear the shame most patiently.

speaker: DUKE
text: I’ll teach you how you shall arraign your conscience,

speaker: DUKE
text: And try your penitence, if it be sound

speaker: DUKE
text: Or hollowly put on.

speaker: JULIET
text: I’ll gladly learn.

speaker: DUKE
text: Love you the man that wronged you?

speaker: JULIET
text: Yes, as I love the woman that wronged him.

speaker: DUKE
text: So then it seems your most offenseful act

speaker: DUKE
text: Was mutually committed?

speaker: JULIET
text: Mutually.

speaker: DUKE
text: Then was your sin of heavier kind than his.

speaker: JULIET
text: I do confess it and repent it, father.

speaker: DUKE
text: ’Tis meet so, daughter; but lest you do repent

speaker: DUKE
text: As that the sin hath brought you to this shame,

speaker: DUKE
text: Which sorrow is always toward ourselves, not heaven,

speaker: DUKE
text: Showing we would not spare heaven as we love it,

speaker: DUKE
text: But as we stand in fear—

speaker: JULIET
text: I do repent me as it is an evil,

speaker: JULIET
text: And take the shame with joy.

speaker: DUKE
text: There rest.

speaker: DUKE
text: Your partner, as I hear, must die tomorrow,

speaker: DUKE
text: And I am going with instruction to him.

speaker: DUKE
text: Grace go with you. Benedicite.

speaker: JULIET
text: Must die tomorrow? O injurious love

speaker: JULIET
text: That respites me a life, whose very comfort

speaker: JULIET
text: Is still a dying horror.

speaker: PROVOST
text: ’Tis pity of him.
